title: Ericoides mucosum
intro: Ericoides mucosum, also known as 'Dwarf Birch' or 'Mountain Birch', is a small to medium-sized shrub native to the mountainous regions of East Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, providing relief from various health issues such as fever reduction, anti-inflammatory effects, and antimicrobial activity, which contribute to its benefits in treating coughs and colds. The bioactive constituents of Ericoides mucosum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Ericoides mucosum are decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used in traditional medicine to treat respiratory and gastrointestinal issues.

parts: Ericoides mucosum, also known as Wintergreen, the medicinal parts of this plant are primarily the leaves. The leaves have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat muscle and joint pain, as well as reduce fever.
